title: cleome tenuis subsp. humilis
intro: Cleome tenuis subsp. humilis, commonly known as the dwarf spider flower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Cleomaceae family, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and slender stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Cleome tenuis subsp. humilis include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and aiding in the healing process of wounds. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
